title: A lot of credit goes to LegsV. I only built on top of his base work. This list has been further expanded with my own experience and the knowledge and feedback from the general, so it's a group effort. I'm just the gonk who took the time to write it down.

I you are familiar with Mod Organizer2, it fully works with Cyberpunk 2077 and I highly recommend using it. Manual install is obviously fine but Mo2 will make things easier and offers extra features like having different mod profiles per character. The main install folder of cyberpunk is never touched, all mods run from another location on your drive so it's literally impossible to break your game.
description: With Mo2 you can easily disable/enable mods with a checkmark, installing is an auto-download from nexus and doubleclicking. It's a timesaver and it will save you some headaches if you don't organize your mods well with a manual installation. It also makes updating and checking for updates easier.

title: >Shut down mo2
description: >Download and install Root Builder (dl and unzip in your MO2's plugin folder)

title: >If it checks out, continue building your modlist. Test it as you go forward
description: There is also Vortex which should be compatible with Cyberpunk. I've never tried it myself. Be wary of vortex however as it is known for not always handling conflicting files the way you'd want it too.
